questions.q:
Let f:2,4→ℝ be a differentiable function such that xlogexf'x+logexfx+fx≥1,x∈2,4 with f2=12 and f4=12.
Consider the following two statements:

(A) fx≤1, for all x∈2,4

(B) fx≥1/8, for all x∈2,4

Then,
options: Neither statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>)</mo></math> nor statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is true
questions.solution:
Given,

Domain and range of function,

f:2,4→ℝ

xlogexf'x+logexfx+fx≥1, x∈2,4

⇒xlogexddxfx+logexfxddxx+xfxddxlogex≥1

⇒ddxxlnxfx≥1

⇒ddxxlnxfx≥ddxx

⇒ddxxlnxfx-x≥0

Hence, h(x)=xlnxfx-x is a increasing function,

∴h(x)≥h(2),x∈[2,4]

⇒xlnx×fx-x≥2ln2×f2-2

⇒xlnxf(x)-x≥ln2-2

Similarly, hx≤h4

⇒xlnxfx-x≤ln4-4

So, ln2-2xlnx+1lnx≤f(x)≤ln4-4xlnx+1lnx

Now for x∈2,4

ln4-4xlnx+1lnx≤ln4-42ln2+1ln2=1-1ln2<1

⇒fx≤1 for x∈2,4

Now for x∈2,4

ln2-2xlnx+1lnx≥ln2-24ln4+1ln4=ln2-24ln4+1ln4=18+14ln2>18

Hence, f(x)≥18

Hence both A & B are correct.
Note this question was bonus in Jee Main 2023 April session, as LMVT on f(x)·xlnx can't be satisfied.

Hence, no such f(x) exist.

questions.q: The set of all a∈ℝ for which the equation x|x-1|+|x+2|+a=0 has exactly one real root, is
questions.solution:
Given,

x|x-1|+|x+2|+a=0

Now taking, Case I: x<-2, we get

-x2+x-x-2+a=0

⇒a=x2+2

And y=x2+2 is decreasing ∀x∈(-∞,-2)

Now taking Case II: -2≤x<1 we get,

-x2+x+x+2+a=0

⇒a=x2-2x-2

And y=x2-2x-2 

So, dydx=2x-1≤0 ∀ x∈[-2,1)

Hence, y is decreasing ∀x∈[-2,1)

Now taking Case III: x≥1 we get,

x2-x+x+2+a=0

⇒a=-x2+2

And y=-x2+2 is decreasing ∀x∈[1,∞)

Hence, from all the cases we can say that nature of function is continuously decreasing so, it will cut x-axis only one time,

∴ Exactly one real root ∀ a∈R

Given, f1+f2=f3

Now possible values of f3 are 2,3,4,5 and 6 (it cannot be 1 as minimum value f1 & f2 is 1, so 1+1=2 )

For each k, if f3=k then there will be k-1 set of values for f1 and f2

For example if f3=3 then possible value of f1 & f2 will be 1,2 or 2,1⇒ 2 values 

Similarly for f3=4, possible values will be 1,3,3,1,2,2⇒3 values.

And so on.

So number of possible combinations of f1, f2 and f3 are 1+2+3+4+5=15

And for each value of f3 there will be 6 possible value of f4, so total cases will be 15×6=90

We know that r≤r<r+1

nr≤nr<nr+1

On adding all the above inequalities, we get

r+2r+…+nr≤r+2r+...+nr<r+2r+...nr+n

Using 1+2+3+...+n=nn+12, n∈N,

nn+12·r≤r+2r+...+nr<nn+12·r+n

⇒nn+12·rn2≤r+2r+...+nrn2<nn+12·r+nn2

Now, limn→∞n(n+1)·r2·n2=limn→∞n21+1n·r2·n2=r2

and limn→∞nn+12·r+nn2=limn→∞n21+1n·r+1n2·n2=r2

questions.q: Define a relation R over a class of n×n real matrices A and B as "ARB iff there exists a non-singular matrix P such that PAP-1=B". Then which of the following is true ?
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is symmetric, transitive but not reflexive
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is reflexive, symmetric but not transitive
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is reflexive, transitive but not symmetric
questions.solution:
Given A and B are matrices of n×n order and ARB iff there exists a non-singular matrix P detP≠0 such that PAP-1=B.

Reflexivity Check :

ARA⇒PAP-1=A which is true for P=I.

So, R is reflexive relation.

Symmetric Check :

ARB⇒PAP-1=B⇒P-1PAP-1P=P-1BP⇒IAI=P-1BP⇒P-1BP=A⇒BRA for matrix P-1.

So, R is symmetric relation.

Transitivity Check :

ARB⇒PAP-1=B and BRC⇒PBP-1=C.

So, PPAP-1P-1=C.

⇒P2AP2-1=C⇒ARC

So, R is a transitive relation.

Since, R is reflexive, symmetric and transitive all.

Hence, R is an equivalence relation.

C=(x,y)∈Z×Z:(x−2)2+(y−2)2≤4
If the total number of relations from A∩B to A∩C is 2p, then the value of p is:
questions.solution:
A=(x,y)∈Z×Z;(x−2)2+y2≤4

B=(x,y)≤Z×Z;x2+y2≤4

C=(x,y)∈Z×Z;(x−2)2+(y−2)2≤4

Here, we can see from the above diagram A∩B={(0,0),(1,0),(2,0),(1,1),(1,-1)}⇒n(A∩B)=5

Similarly n(A∩C)=5

Relation from A∩B to A∩C=25×5=2p

⇒p=25

Define the relations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mfenced><mi>S</mi></mfenced></math> as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mi>B</mi></math> if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>A</mi><mo>∩</mo><msup><mi>B</mi><mi>c</mi></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo>∪</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>B</mi><mo>∩</mo><msup><mi>A</mi><mi>c</mi></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>ϕ</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi></math> if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>∪</mo><msup><mi>B</mi><mi>c</mi></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>∪</mo><msup><mi>A</mi><mi>c</mi></msup><mo>,</mo><mo>∀</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>P</mi><mfenced><mi>S</mi></mfenced></math> . Then :

Given,

AR1B⇒A∩Bc∪Ac∩B=ϕ

Now for reflexive property, replacing B with A we get,

A∩Ac∪Ac∩A which ϕ always,

So, B∩Ac∪Bc∩A which is same as A∩Bc∪Ac∩B, hence the relation is symmetric,

Now checking for transitive
A∩Bc∪Ac∩B=ϕ;

a∪b which is given as empty set ϕ

Hence, we can say that, a=ϕ=b⇒A=B

∴  A=C equivalence.

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> be a relation defined on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">ℕ</mi></math> as a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> b is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>b</mi></math> is a multiple of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">ℕ</mi></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Given:

R=a,b:2a+3b is divisible by 5 for a,b∈N

Reflexive:

2a+3a=5a is always divisible by 5.

So, a,a∈R

Symmetric:

Let a,b∈R i.e., 2a+3b=5λ, where λ∈Z+.

Now,

5a+5b=multiple of 5

⇒2a+3b+2b+3a=multiple of 5

⇒5λ+2b+3a=multiple of 5

⇒2b+3a=multiple of 5-5λ

So, 2b+3a is divisible by 5

Transitive:

Let a,b, b,c∈R, then

2a+3b=5λ1; λ1∈Z+

2b+3c=5λ2; λ2∈Z+

So,

2a+5b+3c=5λ1+λ2

⇒2a+3c=5λ1+λ2-b

Hence, 2a+3c is divisible by 5, so a,c∈R

Hence, R is an equivalence relation.

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> be a relation on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>N</mi><mo>×</mo><mi>N</mi></math> defined by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>R</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>d</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>  if and only if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mi>d</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>b</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>c</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>c</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>d</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is
options: symmetric but neither reflexive nor transitive
transitive but neither reflexive nor symmetric
reflexive and symmetric but not transitive
symmetric and transitive but not reflexive
questions.solution:
Given, a,bRc,d⇒adb-c=bca-d

⇒a,bRc,d⇔b-cbc=a-dad

⇒1c-1b=1d-1a

⇒1a-1b=1d-1c

Reflexive:

a,bRa,b⇒1a-1b=1b-1a

It is false. So, given relation is not reflexive.

Symmetric:

a,bRc,d⇒1a-1b=1d-1c

⇒1c-1d=1b-1a

∴c,dRa,b

It is symmetric.

Transitive:

a,bRc,d⇒1a-1b=1d-1c

c,dRe,f=1c-1d=1e-1f

1a-1b=1e-1f

⇒a,bRe,f

So, not transitive.

questions.q: In a group of 100 persons 75 speak English and 40 speak Hindi. Each person speaks at least one of the two languages. If the number of persons who speak only English is α and the number of persons who speaks only Hindi is β, then the eccentricity of the ellipse 25β2x2+α2y2=α2β2 is
questions.solution:
Given,

In a group of 100 persons 75 speak English and 40 speak Hindi, 

And each person speaks at least one of the two languages,

And the number of persons who speak only English is α and the number of persons who speaks only Hindi is β,

Now from above diagram we can see that,

β=100-75=25

So, α=75-40-25=60

Hence, number of people who speak English only is α=60 and Hindi is β=25,

Now putting the value in given equation of ellipse we get,

25x2602+y2252=1

⇒ x236×4+y225=1

Now comparing with  x2a2+y2b2=1 we get, a=12 & b=5

Now we know that, 

Eccentricity of ellipse is given by e=1-b2a2

⇒e=1-2536×4=11912
